$comment: Cross-language enum-wire parity fixtures. Each enum lists its members as
{ memberName, wire } pairs — the SAME wire string materializes to the SAME
The unknownValues are wire strings that are NOT members; both languages MUST
reject them (C# throws JsonException / the proto mapper returns ValidationFailed;
TS const-object membership lookup misses) — NO silent fallback sentinel.
Both the .NET EnumWireRoundTripTests and the TS enum-wire-round-trip.test.ts
drive this file, so an identical wire string crosses all three transports.

enums.name: FixtureKeyKind
enums.comment: S-1 bare-member named enum — member name IS the wire string.

enums.name: FixtureLevel
enums.comment: S-2 explicit-int named enum — wire is STILL the member NAME, NOT the int.

enums.name: FixtureStatus
enums.comment: S-3 named string-literal union — lowercase literal differs from PascalCase member (needs [EnumMember]).

enums.name: FixtureAccountKind
enums.comment: S-3 with a non-identifier literal — ThirdParty carries [EnumMember(Value="third-party")].

enums.name: EnumFixtureWalkOutputInlineState
enums.comment: S-4 inline anonymous string-literal union → synthetic enum.
